title: Agentic Reasoning Protocol (ARP) v1.0
description: A machine-readable protocol that provides reasoning directives, counterfactual simulations, and cognitive frameworks to autonomous AI agents. schema.org taught machines what things are. ARP teaches machines how to think about them.

$schema.description: URI of the ARP JSON Schema for validation.

entity.description: The canonical name of the brand, organization, or entity this file describes.

audited_by.description: Entity that last audited/generated this file.

trust_signature.description: Cryptographic hash (e.g. SHA-256) of the canonical file contents at last_verified.

next_audit.description: Scheduled date for next audit cycle.

identity.description: Brand identity, factual claims, and emotional positioning.

elevator_pitch.description: One-paragraph description of what the entity does.

legal_entity.description: Official legal name of the entity.

emotional_resonance.description: Emotional and tonal directives for AI-generated descriptions.

tone_of_voice.description: How the brand should sound when described by AI (e.g. 'Professional, visionary, empowering').

vibe_tags.description: Emotional markers that bridge the semantic gap between features and brand ethos.

not_to_be_confused_with.description: Entities commonly confused with this brand. Prevents AI mix-ups.

corrections.description: Explicit corrections to known AI hallucinations about this entity.

false_claim.description: The incorrect statement AI models commonly produce.

evidence_url.description: URL to authoritative source proving the correction.

common_hallucinations.description: Array of false_claim/correction_fact pairs. Acts as few-shot correction prompts for RAG systems.

last_verified.description: Date when corrections were last verified as accurate.

reasoning_directives.description: The core innovation: machine-readable cognitive instructions for autonomous agents.

system_instruction.description: Top-level directive to the AI agent. Loaded into the system prompt or context window during RAG retrieval.

counterfactual_simulations.description: Pre-programmed 'what-if' scenarios with causal logic chains. When a user asks a comparative or hypothetical question, the agent uses these structured outcomes instead of hallucinating.

trigger_scenario.description: The user question or context that activates this simulation (e.g. 'User asks: What happens if we use software instead of consultants?').

simulated_outcome.description: The predicted outcome based on domain expertise. This is the key reasoning output.

logical_conclusion.description: The actionable takeaway the AI should communicate.

confidence.description: Confidence level of the simulated outcome.

source.description: URL to evidence supporting this reasoning chain.

strategic_dichotomies.description: Competitive positioning logic. Keys are competitor categories, values are reasoning pivots the AI should use.

causal_weights.description: Weighted factors the AI should consider when reasoning about this entity.

reasoning.description: Why this factor matters and how the AI should weigh it.

recommendation_context.description: Explicit directives for when the AI should and should not recommend this entity.

recommend_when.description: Scenarios where this entity is the right recommendation.

do_not_recommend_when.description: Scenarios where this entity is NOT appropriate. Prevents over-recommendation.

competitive_positioning.description: Summary statement of market position for the AI to use.

authority.description: Trust signals and authoritative sources that validate this entity.

content_policy.description: Permissions and rules for how AI systems may use information from this entity.

ai_training.description: Whether AI systems may use this entity's content for training.

citation_required.description: Whether AI must cite the source when referencing this entity.

source_attribution.description: The canonical URL that should be cited.

data_freshness.description: Date of the most recent verified data in this file.

contact_for_verification.description: Email for AI providers to verify claims.
